headline: TIER-ONE LITHIUM LANDS IN QUEBEC: Q2 Metals Posts 295Mt at 1.36% Li₂O at Cisco — Among the World's Largest Hard Rock Deposits
snippet: Q2 Metals (TSXV: QTWO) released its inaugural inferred resource for the Cisco Lithium Project in James Bay, Quebec: 295 million tonnes at 1.36% Li₂O (9.9 Mt LCE combined), placing it among the world's largest hard rock lithium deposits — just 6.5km from paved highway and rail access in Canada's top-ranked James Bay mining jurisdiction.

headline: ZIJIN'S GOLD AND LITHIUM MACHINE DOUBLES: Q1 2026 Net Profit Hits $3.5B as Gold Output Jumps 23% and Lithium Production Rockets 10x Year-Over-Year
snippet: Zijin Mining Group reported Q1 2026 net profit of 25.2 billion yuan (+101.9% YoY) on revenue of 98.5 billion yuan (+24.8%), powered by a 23% surge in gold output and lithium carbonate production rising tenfold year-over-year — a real-time benchmark for what diversified gold-lithium producers can earn at current commodity prices.

headline: LITHIUM'S COMMERCIAL RIVAL ARRIVES: Bloomberg Reports Sodium-Ion Batteries Are Now a "Credible Alternative" as CATL and BYD Scale to GWh Production
snippet: Volatile lithium prices and maturing sodium-ion chemistry have pushed the technology from lab curiosity to commercial reality in 2026, with CATL and BYD ramping GWh-scale production; cells show a clear advantage in extreme cold weather and are approaching cost parity with lithium-ion — a structural demand threat lithium mining investors need to price in.

headline: WEDBUSH PUTS $90 ON MP MATERIALS: Vertical Integration at Mountain Pass Called America's Most Underappreciated Rare Earth Asset
snippet: Wedbush initiated MP Materials (NYSE: MP) at Outperform with a $90 target — versus the current ~$64 — citing the Mountain Pass mine-to-magnet vertical integration with Apple and GM as anchor customers, and a $200M Texas magnet facility set to break ground this year as the most underpriced dimension of the MP story; the stock is up 27% YTD.

headline: $4.6 BILLION INTO URANIUM ETFS IN 2025: Why the Nuclear Bull Market Has Moved Beyond Sentiment Into Structural Demand — And Isn't Done
snippet: The uranium ETF complex absorbed $4.6 billion in 2025 inflows, delivering a 156% one-year return as AI data center power demand and IRA credits drove utilities to lock in multi-decade supply contracts; with NexGen's Rook I mine (30M lbs/year capacity) entering construction this summer, the supply-side case for the sector is gaining real-world confirmation.
